Ghostty now supports fonts with mixed color/non-color glyphs and I believe is the only terminal to do so correctly. To detect it I had to write my own font table parsers since CoreText/FreeType both only have face-level colorization detection. See PR notes below for details.

Unpopular opinion: you should copy/fork/DIY your dependencies for everything but the most complicated or sensitive functionality (GUI, crypto, networking, etc.). Blindly depending on trivial functionality or having a deep dependency tree causes more problems than it solves.

In what is definitely the strongest signal I've ever received by a VERY wide margin on the level of industry standardization Terraform has achieved: my FAA examiner (a 30+ yr experienced professional pilot) knew about and uses Terraform for some "side stuff." Unbelievable.
For the next 24 hours or so I’m officially the worlds newest SF50 type rated pilot. Passed!

My opinion on the whole terminal AI thing: I think iTerm's implementation of it is pretty unoffensive, but I also don't plan on adding native AI integrations into Ghostty. My hope/plan is to enable this sort of behavior via a plugin interface instead.

Good morning to everyone except the flight scheduler which assigned me a 5 AM flight. Waking up at 4 AM and diving immediately into systems discussions and training flights is rough. Silver lining I’m done for the day at 11 AM.

I've had "4166" written on a piece of paper on my desk for a couple months. Many will recognize the value as the number of microseconds I have per frame for a 240Hz refresh rate. A few months ago a full screen redraw took 36000 microseconds . Today, it takes around 1900us.
